mission.title: Install and Configure Cloudevents on Kubernetes
mission.description: CloudEvents is a specification for describing event data in common formats to provide interoperability across services, platforms, and systems. Installing CloudEvents allows you to standardize event data formats across your applications and services.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the CloudEvents Helm repository to your Helm client to access the CloudEvents chart.
```bash
helm repo add cloudevents https://gitlab.com/api/v4/projects/15775100/packages/helm/stable
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the CloudEvents Helm chart
steps.description:
Install the CloudEvents chart in a new namespace called 'cloudevents'. This will deploy the CloudEvents service.
```bash
helm install cloudevents cloudevents/cloudevents --namespace cloudevents --create-namespace --version 0.13.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the CloudEvents pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cloudevents
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the CloudEvents deployment to ensure optimal resource usage.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/cloudevents --namespace cloudevents --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the CloudEvents release from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall cloudevents --namespace cloudevents
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with CloudEvents.
```bash
kubectl delete crd cloudevents.cloudevents.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'cloudevents' namespace to clean up all resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace cloudevents
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and resources.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace cloudevents -o yaml > cloudevents-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the CloudEvents chart.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the CloudEvents release
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the CloudEvents release to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade cloudevents cloudevents/cloudevents --namespace cloudevents --version 0.13.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the CloudEvents pods are running successfully after the upgrade.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cloudevents
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If the CloudEvents pods are stuck in Pending, check for resource availability in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace cloudevents
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the CloudEvents service is not reachable, check the service configuration and endpoints.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace cloudevents
kubectl describe svc cloudevents --namespace cloudevents
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace cloudevents
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of CloudEvents will have the pods running in the 'cloudevents' namespace without errors. You should be able to access the CloudEvents service as configured.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add cloudevents https://gitlab.com/api/v4/projects/15775100/packages/helm/stable
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and Helm installed to deploy CloudEvents.
